content:
# 1 Corinthians Chapter 2 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/1CO_002_page_001.jpg

1. And I, having come to you, brothers, came not according to excellence of speech or of wisdom, announcing to you the mystery of God.

2. For I determined not to know anything among you except Yeshua Christos, and this [one] crucified.

3. And I was with you in weakness and in fear and in much trembling,

4. and the speech of me and the proclamation of me [were] not in persuasive words of wisdom but in demonstration of [the] Spirit and of power,

5. that the faith of you might not be in [the] wisdom of men but in [the] power of God.

6. And wisdom we speak among the mature, but wisdom not of this age nor of the rulers of this age, the [ones] being brought to nothing;

7. but we speak [the] wisdom of God in [a] mystery, the [one] having been hidden, which God predetermined before the ages for the glory of us,

8. which none of the rulers of this age knew; for if they had known, they would not have crucified the Lord of the glory.

9. But just as it has been written, "What eye did not see and ear did not hear and upon [the] heart of man did not arise, what God prepared for the [ones] loving him."

10. But to us God revealed [them] through the Spirit; for the Spirit searches all [things], even the deep [things] of God.

11. For who among men knows the [things] of the man except the spirit of the man, the [one] in him? So also the [things] of God no one knows except the Spirit of God.

12. And we received not the spirit of the world but the Spirit, the [one] from God, that we might know the [things] graciously given to us by God;

13. which [things] also we speak, not in taught words of human wisdom but in [words] taught of [the] Spirit, interpreting spiritual [things] to spiritual [persons].

14. But [the] natural man does not receive the [things] of the Spirit of God, for they are foolishness to him, and he is not able to know [them], because they are spiritually discerned.

15. But the spiritual [one] discerns all [things], but he himself is discerned by no one.

16. "For who knew the mind of [the] Lord? Who will instruct him?" But we have the mind of Christos.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.1 — "mystery of God" (μυστήριον τοῦ θεοῦ):** Sinaiticus reads μυστήριον rather than μαρτύριον ("testimony"). This is a significant variant — "mystery" connects to the hidden wisdom theme of vv.6-10 and to the cosmic mystery tradition.

2. **v.4 — "demonstration of the Spirit and of power":** ἀπόδειξις — a technical rhetorical term meaning "proof." Paul ironically uses the language of rhetoric to describe what is beyond rhetoric.

3. **v.8 — "the Lord of the glory" (τὸν κύριον τῆς δόξης):** One of Paul's most exalted christological titles. The rulers crucified the one who bears divine glory.

4. **v.9 — The citation:** Not an exact quotation from any known source. Closest parallels are Isaiah 64:4 and 65:17. Some early sources attributed it to the Apocalypse of Elijah.

5. **v.13 — πνευματικοῖς πνευματικά:** The dative πνευματικοῖς could be masculine ("to spiritual persons") or neuter ("by spiritual means"). The ambiguity may be intentional.

6. **v.16 — Isaiah 40:13 (LXX):** Paul's concluding citation. "But we have the mind of Christ" — νοῦν Χριστοῦ. The community possesses what Isaiah said no one could possess: access to the divine mind.

— EMET, AI Translator
